response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong technical leadership, software craftsmanship, and experience with large-scale distributed systems (Dunnhumby's millions of requests/second, AWS infrastructure, Kubernetes), he falls significantly short on the critical minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ programming—his resume shows only ~6 months of C++ work at National Instruments in 2014. His recent career trajectory emphasizes full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than systems-level programming or cloud compute platform engineering, which are core to this role.
strengths: Proven technical leadership at Staff level with mentoring experience and ability to establish engineering practices and patterns across teams
Extensive large-scale systems experience including microservices handling millions of requests/second, real-time low-latency applications, and Kubernetes-based infrastructure
Strong software craftsmanship emphasis with demonstrated expertise in TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and architectural design across multiple domains
concerns: Critically deficient C++ experience (~6 months vs. 8-year minimum requirement) despite strong systems background in other languages
Recent career focus on full-stack web development (TypeScript/Node.js) rather than systems programming or cloud compute platform infrastructure
No demonstrated experience with core cloud compute platform concerns such as compute scheduling, resource management, or container runtime systems at architectural level

key_factors.factor: Large-scale distributed systems and infrastructure experience
Technical leadership capability and mentoring skills
